answer: Roots: naha ‘see’, jama ‘hear’, pinima ‘heal’, chapara ‘visit’, napo ‘find’. Derivational pieces: chaji ‘make’, pira ‘want’, manaha ‘again’. Object suffixes: -ri ‘him’, -ro ‘her’, -rona ‘them (f.)’, -nana ‘them (m.)’, -ai ‘you (sg.)’, -mano ‘me’, -âî ‘us’, and allomorphs for 2pl like -râî/-mâî with clitic isa. Negation is aa- before the subject. Subject markers include ne- ‘I’ (positive), a(w)- ‘we’, pu- ‘you (sg.)’, u- ‘you (pl.)’, i-/r- ‘he’, re- ‘they (m.)’, ru- ‘she/they (f.)’. Objects attach to the last verbal element (root, pira, or after manaha).
